top_signals.title: Shipping groups want mines cleared, TSS restored for normal Hormuz traffic
top_signals.summary: Maritime traffic via the Strait of Hormuz will only return to normal levels when mines are removed and traditional routes restored, some of the world’s largest shipping organizations said June 18 while calling for more clarity on transit rules and coordination among authorities to allow safe passage. Iran has warned of mine risks for transiting ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: US-Iran deal to reopen Strait of Hormuz— but full container shipping recovery at least three months away
top_signals.summary: The US-Iran deal should allow a return of container shipping to the Strait of Hormuz, but the scale of disruption caused by the blockade means, even a best-case scenario, puts a recovery of ocean supply chain networks at mid-September 2026 and spot rates rising for at least another four weeks before the market peaks. “This ...

top_signals.title: China’s Farm Drones: A Trojan Horse Washington Overlooks
top_signals.summary:
In 2024, Emilian Kavalski and Claris Diaz argued in “Beyond TikTok – The National Security Risks of Chinese Agricultural Drones” that the national debate over foreign social media platforms risked becoming too narrow, potentially causing Washington to overlook other foreign technologies embedded in critical systems. Two years later, we asked them to revisit their arguments.Image: MB-one via Wikimedia CommonsIn your 2024 article, you argued that TikTok’s data collection risks were overhyped and that fixating on them distracted the United States from more serious Chinese technology threats, like agricultural drones. Two years and several TikTok ban cycles later, has your view

The post China’s Farm Drones: A Trojan Horse Washington Overlooks appeared first on War on the Rocks.

top_signals.title: Dry Bulk Market: China’s Iron Ore Demand Stronger in 2026
top_signals.summary: China’s iron ore imports so far in 2026 have been on the rise compared to last year. In its latest weekly report, shipbroker Banchero Costa said that “2025 was another positive year for global seaborne iron ore trade. top_signals.title: PhilaPort Ranked Nation’s Most Productive Container Port for Third Consecutive Year
top_signals.summary: PhilaPort has once again been named the most productive container port in the United States, according to the 2025 Container Port Productivity Index (CPPI), published by the World Bank and Standard & Poor’s (S&P). Widely recognized as the premier global benchmark for container port efficiency, the CPPI evaluates ports based on operational performance metrics that ...

top_signals.title: Baltic Dry Index Up to 1-Week High
top_signals.summary: The Baltic Exchange’s dry bulk freight index, which monitors rates for ships carrying dry bulk commodities, was up for a second session on Friday, rising about 2.4% to its highest since June 12 at 2,722 points, underpinned by the larger vessel segment. The capesize index, which typically transports 150,000-ton cargoes including iron ore and coal, ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.summary: For many years, World Container Index (WCI) has been the go-to, independent, global reference for index-linked contracts.
